 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","title":"Implement crash-safe checkpointing and deterministic replay cursor semantics","description":"## Context
Indexer and replay systems require reliable continuation after process restarts/crashes. We need explicit checkpoints and idempotent resume semantics from storage.

## Scope
Implement write/checkpoint metadata and restart replay protocol so downstream consumers can resume from last committed offset without duplication or gaps.

## Deliverables
- Durable checkpoint format and atomic update protocol
- Resume/read APIs for indexers and replayers
- Duplicate suppression strategy where at-least-once replay is unavoidable
- Crash/restart integration tests

## Why this matters
Without deterministic replay/resume semantics, indexing quality and forensic trustworthiness degrade quickly.

## Acceptance
- Restart from crash resumes at deterministic point
- Replay APIs provide no silent gap behavior
- Duplicate handling strategy is explicit and tested","notes":"Claimed to complete replay/checkpoint semantics after wa-oegrb.3.3 closure; fixing partial recorder_storage replay API breakage and adding crash/resume coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:52:59.228272-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:52:59.228272-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented crash-safe checkpoint resume cursor semantics with deterministic replay API and tests","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","reliability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.393544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435384Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Implemented missing replay/checkpoint semantics in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs: added ReplayCursor/ReplayBatch/ReplayResumePlan contract types, trait surface (plan_resume/replay_batch), append-log replay reader with deterministic ordinal cursoring and torn-tail-safe behavior, and crash/restart resume tests. Also wired health() to include pipeline metrics default to satisfy expanded health schema. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets (passes); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core recorder_storage -- --nocapture (8 recorder_storage tests passed, including new resume/replay tests).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:52:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","title":"Define retention, partitioning, and archival lifecycle for append-only recorder data","description":"## Context
Full-fidelity recorder data can grow rapidly. Retention and partitioning choices must be first-class so we do not accumulate unbounded costs or operational risk.

## Scope
Define and implement retention windows, partition strategy (time/size), and archival/export hooks for append log segments.

## Deliverables
- Retention policy model (default + override scopes)
- Segment lifecycle logic (active, sealed, archived, purged)
- Tooling hooks for safe archival/export
- Tests proving retention operations preserve invariants and auditability

## Why this matters
Cost and operability determine whether this feature can remain always-on in real swarm usage.

## Acceptance
- Retention behavior is deterministic and observable
- Purge/archive operations do not corrupt offset continuity semantics
- Operators can reason about storage growth via documented policy","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.606628Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.479191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.60657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","title":"Add deep storage-pipeline telemetry, diagnostics, and SLO-oriented metrics","description":"## Context
The writer path is mission-critical; without deep telemetry we cannot tune or safely roll out. We need first-class metrics and tracing for throughput, lag, failures, and durability state.

## Scope
Instrument storage pipeline with metrics/events/logging suitable for SLOs and incident triage.

## Deliverables
- Metrics for append rate, batch size, queue depth, flush latency, error classes, replay lag
- Structured diagnostics for backend health and checkpoint progression
- Alert recommendations for critical degradation patterns
- Documentation mapping metrics to likely remediation actions

## Why this matters
Observability is necessary both for performance tuning and for proving recorder trustworthiness in incidents.

## Acceptance
- Critical failure/lag states are externally visible
- Metrics are stable enough for automated thresholds
- Documentation allows operators to diagnose common failure modes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.570008Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","observability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.523344Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.569934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.4","title":"Indexing track: Tantivy lexical indexing, incremental ingestion, rebuild tooling","description":"## Track objective
Design and implement lexical indexing over recorder logs using Tantivy with near-real-time incremental ingestion and robust rebuild/repair workflows.

## Why this track exists
The recorder only creates value if data is rapidly discoverable. Lexical search is the baseline reliability layer and must remain available regardless of semantic subsystem state.

## Deliverables
- Tantivy schema and analyzer strategy
- Incremental indexer pipeline from append-only log
- Query semantics for phrase, boolean, field filters, time windows
- Rebuild/checkpoint/repair tools

## Acceptance criteria
- Lexical search latency and correctness targets are documented and test-enforced.
- Index can be rebuilt from append-only logs without data ambiguity.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.755958Z","labels":["indexing","lexical","search","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.755747Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.4","title":"Indexing track: Tantivy lexical indexing, incremental ingestion, rebuild tooling","description":"## Track objective
Design and implement lexical indexing over recorder logs using Tantivy with near-real-time incremental ingestion and robust rebuild/repair workflows.

## Why this track exists
The recorder only creates value if data is rapidly discoverable. Lexical search is the baseline reliability layer and must remain available regardless of semantic subsystem state.

## Deliverables
- Tantivy schema and analyzer strategy
- Incremental indexer pipeline from append-only log
- Query semantics for phrase, boolean, field filters, time windows
- Rebuild/checkpoint/repair tools

## Acceptance criteria
- Lexical search latency and correctness targets are documented and test-enforced.
- Index can be rebuilt from append-only logs without data ambiguity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:49:49.68422-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T17:49:49.68422-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children closed: schema design (4.1), incremental ingestion (4.2), commit/merge policy (4.3), reindex/backfill tooling (4.4), lexical query service (4.5), quality harness (4.6). Full lexical indexing track complete.","labels":["indexing","lexical","search","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.755747Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","title":"Design versioned Tantivy schema for recorder text + metadata filters","description":"## Context
Lexical retrieval quality and index stability depend on getting schema decisions right early (field structure, analyzers, stored vs indexed fields, offset references, metadata filters).

## Scope
Design the canonical Tantivy schema for recorder events, including text fields, normalized metadata, and time/agent/pane filters.

## Deliverables
- Versioned Tantivy schema definition with migration notes
- Field-by-field rationale (why indexed/stored/tokenized)
- Analyzer/tokenizer strategy tuned for terminal text (commands, stack traces, mixed symbols)
- Golden tests for schema compatibility and queryability

## Why this matters
Schema drift or poor tokenization destroys search quality and makes reindexing expensive.

## Acceptance
- Schema supports required filters and retrieval use-cases
- Analyzer choices are justified with examples
- Versioning strategy is documented for future migrations","notes":"Delivered Tantivy lexical schema contract in docs/flight-recorder/tantivy-schema-v1.md, including field-by-field schema, tokenizer strategy for terminal text/symbols, migration/rebuild semantics, and golden compatibility/queryability test plan. Linked contract from ADR and cross-project extraction matrix for downstream implementation beads.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.46742Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.243622-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:27:34.625042-05:00","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.46742Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.826595Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.855245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.883639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","title":"Implement incremental Tantivy ingestion from append-log checkpoints","description":"## Context
Recorder writes are append-only; indexer must consume offsets incrementally with deterministic resume behavior and bounded lag.

## Scope
Implement incremental index ingestion from recorder checkpoints, including idempotent retries and commit cadence controls.

## Deliverables
- Offset-driven incremental indexer pipeline
- Checkpoint coordination with storage replay cursors
- Commit/flush policy for freshness vs throughput tradeoffs
- Integration tests for restart/resume without data loss

## Why this matters
Incremental indexing is the bridge between high-throughput logging and usable near-real-time search.

## Acceptance
- Indexer resumes deterministically after restarts
- No silent drops/duplication under normal retry paths
- Lag is measurable and bounded under target load","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented incremental Tantivy ingestion pipeline: AppendLogReader (binary log scanning, seek-to-ordinal, torn-tail safe), IndexDocumentFields (all 24 schema fields), map_event_to_document for all 4 event types with redaction handling, IndexWriter trait for pluggable backends, IncrementalIndexer with checkpoint-coordinated batching/resume/dedup, IndexerLagMonitor. 40 tests covering cold start, resume from checkpoint, multi-pane, mixed types, schema mismatch skip, commit failure propagation, lag monitoring.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.652992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.911617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","title":"Tune Tantivy commit/merge policy for sustained recorder ingest and query latency","description":"## Context
Tantivy segment management strongly affects write amplification and query latency. We need explicit merge and commit strategies aligned with recorder workload.

## Scope
Tune and codify segment lifecycle policy (commit frequency, merge heuristics, memory budgets) for terminal transcript data.

## Deliverables
- Merge policy configuration and rationale
- Benchmarked commit/merge profiles for common load regimes
- Operational toggles for safe runtime tuning
- Tests/benchmarks guarding against pathological segment explosion

## Why this matters
Poor segment strategy can make a correct index unusably slow or expensive.

## Acceptance
- Query latency and ingest throughput remain within target envelopes
- Segment growth remains bounded/predictable
- Policy is tunable without code surgery","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.530604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:37:07.222087-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:14:43.246326-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in tantivy_policy.rs: LoadRegime, CommitPolicy, MergePolicyConfig, MergePolicyEvaluator, CommitTracker, AdaptivePolicyController. 45 tests pass.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","performance","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.530604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.681399Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","title":"Implement deterministic reindex/backfill tooling from recorder source-of-truth log","description":"## Context
We need deterministic rebuild/backfill tooling for schema evolution, corruption recovery, and historical imports.

## Scope
Create tooling to rebuild lexical index from recorder log ranges, with progress reporting and safety checks.

## Deliverables
- Full reindex command path with resumable checkpoints
- Partial/range backfill support by time/offset
- Integrity checks comparing index docs to source offsets
- Operator runbook for rebuild scenarios

## Why this matters
Without robust rebuild tooling, index incidents become high-risk and long-duration.

## Acceptance
- Rebuild can be run safely on large datasets
- Progress and completion criteria are explicit
- Integrity validation detects missing/corrupt ranges","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:35:07.946144-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T17:35:07.946144-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ReindexPipeline (full reindex + partial backfill) with resumable checkpoints, ordinal/time-range backfill, IntegrityChecker for offset and existence verification. 32 tests.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.711388Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","title":"Build lexical query service over Tantivy with stable filters/ranking/snippets","description":"## Context
Hybrid search still depends on high-quality lexical retrieval as both baseline and fallback. Query behavior must be explicit, testable, and stable.

## Scope
Implement lexical query service (filters, ranking, snippets/highlights, pagination) over the Tantivy index.

## Deliverables
- Query API for lexical search with deterministic sorting/pagination
- Filter support (time, pane, session, agent, direction, tags)
- Highlight/snippet strategy suitable for terminal text
- Relevance regression tests and fixture corpus

## Why this matters
Lexical search is the reliability anchor; semantic rankers should enhance, not replace, this foundation.

## Acceptance
- Lexical results are accurate and predictable
- Filters and pagination semantics are stable
- Fixture-based tests protect behavior from regressions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:26:12.331028-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T17:26:12.331028-05:00","close_reason":"Lexical query service implemented: SearchQuery builder, 17 filter types, cursor pagination, TF scoring with field boosts, snippet extraction, InMemorySearchService reference impl. 47 tests.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","query","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.740105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","title":"Create lexical quality harness with golden queries, relevance assertions, and latency budgets","description":"## Context
Search correctness can silently degrade with tokenizer, schema, or ranking changes. We need explicit evaluation harnesses tied to expected outcomes.

## Scope
Establish lexical search quality harness (golden query set + relevance assertions + latency checks).

## Deliverables
- Golden query dataset derived from realistic terminal workflows
- Relevance assertions (must-hit docs, ordering tolerances)
- Latency budget checks per query class
- CI wiring for regression detection

## Why this matters
A formal harness converts subjective “search feels off” complaints into actionable, reproducible failures.

## Acceptance
- Harness runs in CI/local and fails on quality regressions
- Query corpus covers critical forensic/operator scenarios
- Metrics are baseline-tracked for future improvements","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:35:38.364505-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","testing","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.796937Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","title":"Create lexical quality harness with golden queries, relevance assertions, and latency budgets","description":"## Context

## Scope

## Deliverables

## Why this matters

## Acceptance
- Harness runs in CI/local and fails on quality regressions
- Query corpus covers critical forensic/operator scenarios
- Metrics are baseline-tracked for future improvements","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:49:40.476327-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T17:49:40.476327-05:00","close_reason":"Quality harness with golden queries (forensic + agent-workflow suites), 8 relevance assertion types, per-query-class latency budgets, machine-readable QualityReport for CI. 26 tests.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","testing","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.796937Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5","title":"Semantic track: embeddings + vector retrieval + hybrid rank fusion","description":"## Track objective
Implement semantic retrieval and hybrid lexical+semantic ranking over recorder data, reusing validated patterns from cass/xf where appropriate.

## Why this track exists
Pure lexical search misses semantically similar incidents and prior solutions. Hybrid ranking materially improves operator and agent recall in incident/debug workflows.

## Deliverables
- Embedding/chunking strategy for recorder corpus
- Vector retrieval subsystem and ANN strategy
- Rank-fusion strategy combining lexical and semantic evidence
- Offline relevance evaluation corpus and metrics

## Acceptance criteria
- Hybrid ranking behavior is deterministic enough to test and tune.
- Semantic subsystem can degrade gracefully without breaking lexical search.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792358Z","labels":["cass","hybrid","search","semantic","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","title":"Define embedding provider interface and model-governance strategy","description":"## Context
Semantic retrieval needs a model/provider strategy that balances quality, latency, cost, privacy, and offline operability. This must be explicit before implementation.

## Scope
Define embedding provider interface, model selection rubric, and deployment modes (local/remote) for recorder search.

## Deliverables
- Embedding abstraction API with pluggable providers
- Model selection matrix (quality/latency/cost/privacy tradeoffs)
- Normalization/versioning strategy for embedding vectors
- Operational policy for outages and fallback behavior

## Why this matters
Semantic quality depends as much on model governance as algorithm design. Unclear provider rules create unstable, expensive systems.

## Acceptance
- Provider interface supports swapping implementations without query-layer changes
- Selection rationale is documented and reproducible
- Fallback behavior is deterministic when embeddings unavailable","notes":"Claimed to evaluate and land semantic embedding provider governance contract for wa-prefixed bead parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.226779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:53:30.615043-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:53:30.615043-05:00","close_reason":"Embedding provider governance/interface contract documented and validated in docs/flight-recorder/embedding-provider-governance-contract.md","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.226779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.964617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.006784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.058303Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Validated existing governance contract at docs/flight-recorder/embedding-provider-governance-contract.md against bead deliverables: (1) pluggable provider abstraction API (EmbeddingProvider + Registry), (2) model quality/latency/cost/privacy selection matrix and governance policy, (3) normalization + immutable profile_id versioning contract, and (4) deterministic query/index fallback semantics when semantic path is unavailable. This satisfies acceptance requirements for wa-oegrb.5.1 and unblocks wa-oegrb.5.2/wa-oegrb.5.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:53:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","title":"Implement context-aware chunking/windowing policy for semantic embeddings","description":"## Context
Terminal streams are noisy and irregular. Effective semantic retrieval requires principled chunking/windowing, not naive line-level embeddings.

## Scope
Design and implement chunking policy that preserves context boundaries (session/pane/direction/time) while keeping embedding size manageable.

## Deliverables
- Chunking/windowing algorithm and tunable parameters
- Boundary rules for commands, outputs, errors, and prompt transitions
- Metadata linkage from chunk -> source offsets for replay/audit
- Tests on edge cases (very long outputs, tiny fragments, mixed directions)

## Why this matters
Bad chunking degrades semantic recall/precision and undermines trust in hybrid results.

## Acceptance
- Chunking policy yields reproducible chunks across reindex runs
- Chunk metadata fully traces back to source log offsets
- Edge-case tests demonstrate robust boundary handling","notes":"Claimed to deliver semantic chunking/windowing policy contract + edge-case validation plan and source-offset traceability rules.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.291502Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:53:55.322566-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.291502Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.573241Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.108011Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
